title: Repairing electronics instead of replacing: what is feasible and how to start
subtitle: Battery replacements, screen repairs, and the iFixit-first approach to common faults
excerpt: From October 2021, UK regulations require manufacturers of washing machines, dishwashers, TVs, and lighting to make spare parts available for 7-10 years. Smartphones and laptops are not yet in scope, but a thriving market in parts and repair guides means screen replacements, battery swaps, and charging port repairs are within reach for most people.
sourceNotes: UK Right to Repair Regulations 2021 (SI 2021/1487). iFixit.com repairability scores and repair guides database. Restart Project and Repair Cafe network guidance on common consumer electronics faults.

content.text: The electronics repairs that prevent the most waste are battery replacements in smartphones and laptops, screen replacements in phones, and heating element or pump replacements in washing machines and dishwashers. Battery failure is the single most common reason a phone or laptop is thrown out. All of these have a steady repair market. Parts are available. Step-by-step guides are on iFixit.
Search iFixit.com for your device model before buying parts. The

and the number of available guides tells you what repairs are documented. iFixit also stocks parts for many common devices. Check whether the guide requires specialist tools (pentalobe screwdrivers for Apple products, for example): these cost a few pounds and are reusable.

A smartphone battery that holds less than 80% of its original charge typically needs replacement after 2-3 years of normal use. Battery health can be checked in Settings on iPhone; Android battery health apps give similar information. Replacement batteries for most current smartphones are available from iFixit or specialist phone parts suppliers. Most guides rate battery replacement 4-6 difficulty on a 10-point scale: it requires care but not specialist training.

Repair Cafes. Volunteer-run events in libraries and community centres. Skilled volunteers help repair electronics, clothing, and household items. Repair Cafe International's website lists events by location.
The Restart Project. London-based, with a national network. It focuses on consumer electronics and runs Restart Parties where fixers help with repairs.
Independent mobile phone repair shops. Often offer screen and battery replacement at lower cost than manufacturer service centres. Often done while you wait.
